Petitoin For Determination Of Existence And Validity Of Trust, Appointment Of Trustee, Accounting An
Off Calendar Jan-15-2019 Off Calendar

Petitoin For Determination Of Existence And Validity Of Trust, Appointment Of Trustee, Accounting An
Off Calendar Dec-11-2018 Continued To Jan-15-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petitoin For Determination Of Existence And Validity Of Trust, Appointment Of Trustee, Accounting An
Off Calendar Sep-18-2018 Continued To Dec-11-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Jeffrey Lowenthal for and with Limor Elbaz (Petitioner).
Goli Mahdavir for Citibank.
Lisha Hannifan (Objector) in Propria Persona.

The Court stated that it still has questions as to whether this is a Trust or a contract matter, that it is not sure it is proper in the Probate Department, that there is no dispute that there is a Trust and once again urges Ms. Hannafin to hire an Attorney. Mr. Lowenthal stated that he believes it is a trust read together with the revocable trust which became an irrevocable trust and that a partition action is appropriate in the Probate Department. Ms. Hannafin stated that they are at an impasse as to how to split the property. The Court informed the Parties that the Probate Department will not hear a partition action and questions the possibility of mediation. The Parties agreed to participate in mediation. The Court signed the order referring the matter to Pro Bono Mediation. Mediation must be completed no later than November 30, 2018. The Parties are to notify the Probate Examiner as to the outcome of mediation by December 5, 2018. The Petition for Determination of Existence and Validity of Trust, etc. is continued to and the Parties are to return on December 11, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of mediation.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

Petitoin For Determination Of Existence And Validity Of Trust, Appointment Of Trustee, Accounting An
Off Calendar Jul-17-2018 Continued To Sep-18-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Dana Andreoli and Laura Gildengorin for and with Limor Elbaz.
Alexandra Whitworth for Citibank.
Lisha Hannafin (Respondent) in Propria Persona.

The Court notes that the parties are here to determine if the June 29, 2012 agreement created a Trust on the property for Ellie's interest, questions why this is not a contract matter and how it creates a Trust, and stated that there are other Trusts. Ms. Whitworth stated that Citibank's position is there is a lien on the property and a deed of Trust on the property, and she wants to make sure the parties do not contest the lien. Ms. Andreoli stated that she will go to the Law & Motion Department if a partition action is needed, that they need to determine if there is a Trust and that they need to do some discovery. Ms. Hannafin stated that she objects to this, that this matter should be in Family Law, that she has questions about the agreement, that they bought this property when they were in a relationship and that Ms. Elbaz is being litigious. Ms. Andreoli stated that she thinks the intent is clear on the face of the documents. Ms. Hannafin stated that there is no trust, that the Trust was never funded and that the property was never transferred. The Court encouraged Ms. Hannafin to hire a Lawyer. The Petition for Determination of Existence and Validity of Trust, etc. is continued to September 18, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court stated that the Parties can talk to each other to see if they can work something out and discuss how to proceed.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.
